name: Volodymyr Zelenskyy
bio: President of Ukraine since May 2019. A former comedian and actor, he has led Ukraine through the full-scale Russian invasion since February 2022, becoming a prominent wartime leader and addressing numerous international bodies to rally support for Ukrainian sovereignty.

name: Donald Trump
bio: 45th and 47th President of the United States, serving 2017–2021 and again from January 2025. His platform centers on immigration enforcement, trade protectionism, and an America First foreign policy.

name: Joe Biden
bio: 46th President of the United States (2021–2025). Previously served as Vice President under Obama and represented Delaware in the Senate for 36 years. Focused on COVID-19 recovery, infrastructure, and transatlantic alliances.

name: Vladimir Putin
bio: President of Russia since 2000 (with a brief stint as PM 2008–2012). Former KGB officer who launched the full-scale invasion of Ukraine in February 2022.

name: Xi Jinping
role: President / General Secretary of the CPC
bio: General Secretary of the Chinese Communist Party since 2012 and President since 2013. Has consolidated power to a degree unseen since Mao, eliminating presidential term limits in 2018.

name: Emmanuel Macron
bio: President of France since 2017. Former investment banker who founded En Marche and won re-election in 2022. A leading voice for European strategic autonomy.

name: Narendra Modi
bio: Prime Minister of India since 2014, leading the BJP to three consecutive election victories. Focused on economic modernization and elevating India's global role. Chaired the G20 in 2023.

name: Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva
bio: President of Brazil serving his third term since January 2023 (previously 2003–2010). Former metalworker and union leader who co-founded the Workers' Party. Chaired the G20 in 2024.

name: Cyril Ramaphosa
bio: President of South Africa since 2018. Former trade union leader and key apartheid transition negotiator. Navigating coalition politics after the ANC lost its parliamentary majority in 2024.

name: Javier Milei
bio: President of Argentina since December 2023. Libertarian economist who won office as a political outsider. Known for radical free-market proposals and dramatic rhetoric.

name: Recep Tayyip Erdoğan
bio: President of Turkey since 2014, previously PM from 2003. Has consolidated executive power and positioned Turkey as a regional power broker.

name: Abdel Fattah el-Sisi
bio: President of Egypt since 2014. Former military commander who led the 2013 overthrow of Mohamed Morsi.

name: Yoon Suk-yeol
bio: President of South Korea since May 2022. Former prosecutor general. Faced a martial law crisis in December 2024 and subsequent impeachment.

name: Ferdinand Marcos Jr
bio: President of the Philippines since June 2022. Son of former dictator Ferdinand Marcos. Has strengthened defense ties with the US amid South China Sea tensions.

name: Joko Widodo
bio: President of Indonesia from 2014 to October 2024. Known as Jokowi, the first Indonesian president without a military or political elite background.

name: Prabowo Subianto
bio: President of Indonesia since October 2024. Former military general and defense minister who won the 2024 presidential election.

name: Bola Tinubu
bio: President of Nigeria since May 2023. Former Lagos governor. Embarked on economic reforms including fuel subsidy removal and currency float.

name: William Ruto
bio: President of Kenya since September 2022. Rose from humble origins to the presidency. Focused on digital economy and agricultural reform.

name: Gustavo Petro
bio: President of Colombia since August 2022, the country's first left-wing president. Former guerrilla member and mayor of Bogotá.

name: Gabriel Boric
bio: President of Chile since March 2022, the youngest in Chilean history. Former student protest leader.

name: Andrés Manuel López Obrador
bio: President of Mexico from 2018 to October 2024. Known as AMLO, led the Morena movement with a focus on austerity and social programs.

name: Claudia Sheinbaum
bio: President of Mexico since October 2024, the first woman to hold the office. Former head of government of Mexico City and environmental engineer.

name: Paul Kagame
bio: President of Rwanda since 2000. Led the RPF force that ended the 1994 genocide. Credited with rapid economic development.

name: Andrzej Duda
bio: President of Poland since 2015. Major advocate for NATO's eastern flank reinforcement since Russia's invasion of Ukraine.

name: Ali Khamenei
bio: Supreme Leader of Iran since 1989. Holds ultimate authority over foreign policy, military, and nuclear program.

name: Ebrahim Raisi
bio: President of Iran from 2021 until his death in a helicopter crash in May 2024.

name: Masoud Pezeshkian
bio: President of Iran since July 2024. Reformist-leaning heart surgeon who won a snap election following Raisi's death.

name: Mohammed bin Salman
bio: Crown Prince of Saudi Arabia and de facto ruler since 2017. Driving Vision 2030 economic diversification.

name: Alexander Lukashenko
bio: President of Belarus since 1994. Cracked down on 2020 protests and allowed Russia to use Belarus as a staging ground for the Ukraine invasion.

name: Ahmed al-Sharaa
bio: President of Syria following the fall of the Assad regime in December 2024. Previously known as Abu Mohammed al-Julani.

name: Benjamin Netanyahu
bio: Longest-serving Israeli PM, currently in his sixth government since December 2022. Defined by the October 7, 2023 Hamas attack and subsequent military operations.

name: Rishi Sunak
bio: PM of the United Kingdom from October 2022 to July 2024. First British Asian and Hindu to serve as PM.

name: Keir Starmer
bio: PM of the United Kingdom since July 2024. Former Director of Public Prosecutions. Led Labour to a landslide victory.

name: Justin Trudeau
bio: PM of Canada from 2015 to March 2025. Led the Liberal Party through three election victories before stepping down.

name: Mark Carney
bio: PM of Canada since March 2025. Former Governor of the Bank of Canada and the Bank of England.

name: Anthony Albanese
bio: PM of Australia since May 2022. Labor leader focused on climate action and the AUKUS security partnership.

name: Fumio Kishida
bio: PM of Japan from October 2021 to October 2024. Oversaw Japan's historic defense spending increase.

name: Shigeru Ishiba
bio: PM of Japan since October 2024. Former defense minister known for security policy expertise.

name: Olaf Scholz
bio: Chancellor of Germany since December 2021. Declared a 'Zeitenwende' in German defense policy following Russia's invasion of Ukraine.

name: Giorgia Meloni
bio: PM of Italy since October 2022. First woman to serve as Italian PM. Has taken a firm pro-Ukraine, pro-NATO stance.

name: Pedro Sánchez
bio: PM of Spain since 2018. PSOE leader who has governed through coalition politics.

name: Viktor Orbán
bio: PM of Hungary since 2010. Has championed 'illiberal democracy' and is often at odds with EU institutions.

name: Donald Tusk
bio: PM of Poland since December 2023. Former European Council President who returned to lead a coalition government.

name: Mark Rutte
bio: NATO Secretary General since October 2024. Previously the longest-serving Dutch PM (2010–2024).

name: Kyriakos Mitsotakis
bio: PM of Greece since 2019. Won re-election in 2023 with a strong majority.

name: Anwar Ibrahim
bio: PM of Malaysia since November 2022. Former deputy PM who endured years of imprisonment before his political comeback.

name: Lee Hsien Loong
bio: PM of Singapore from 2004 to May 2024. Son of founding PM Lee Kuan Yew.

name: Lawrence Wong
bio: PM of Singapore since May 2024. Rose to prominence leading COVID-19 response. Represents a generational leadership transition.

name: Christopher Luxon
bio: PM of New Zealand since November 2023. Former Air New Zealand CEO.

name: Abiy Ahmed
bio: PM of Ethiopia since 2018. Won the 2019 Nobel Peace Prize for the Eritrea peace deal.

name: Shehbaz Sharif
bio: PM of Pakistan since March 2024. Brother of former PM Nawaz Sharif. Focused on economic stabilization.

name: Mette Frederiksen
bio: PM of Denmark since 2019. Prominent voice on Greenland sovereignty and increased defense spending.

name: Antony Blinken
bio: U.S. Secretary of State from 2021 to January 2025. Led American diplomacy through the Ukraine crisis and Middle East conflicts.

name: Marco Rubio
bio: U.S. Secretary of State since January 2025. Former Senator from Florida known for hawkish positions on China and Cuba.

name: Sergei Lavrov
bio: Foreign Minister of Russia since 2004. One of the longest-serving foreign ministers globally. The public face of Russian diplomatic positions.

name: Wang Yi
bio: Director of the CPC Central Foreign Affairs Commission and Foreign Minister. Brokered the Saudi-Iran rapprochement in 2023.

name: S. Jaishankar
bio: External Affairs Minister of India since 2019. Former ambassador to the US and China. Articulate defender of India's multi-alignment policy.

name: Annalena Baerbock
bio: Foreign Minister of Germany since 2021. Green Party leader championing 'feminist foreign policy.'

name: David Lammy
bio: Foreign Secretary of the UK since July 2024. Advocates for 'progressive realism' in foreign policy.

name: Josep Borrell
role: Former High Representative for Foreign Affairs
bio: EU High Representative for Foreign Affairs from 2019 to 2024. Coordinated EU sanctions on Russia and military aid to Ukraine.

name: Kaja Kallas
bio: EU High Representative since December 2024. Former PM of Estonia and one of Europe's most hawkish voices against Russia.

name: Hakan Fidan
bio: Foreign Minister of Turkey since 2023. Former head of Turkish intelligence (MIT).

name: Prince Faisal bin Farhan
bio: Foreign Minister of Saudi Arabia since 2019. Overseeing diplomatic normalization efforts.

name: António Guterres
bio: UN Secretary-General since 2017. Former PM of Portugal and UNHCR chief. Focused on climate action and conflict prevention.

name: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus
bio: Director-General of the WHO since 2017. Led the global health response to COVID-19.

name: Ursula von der Leyen
bio: President of the European Commission since 2019, re-elected in 2024. Led the EU response to COVID-19 and the Ukraine crisis.

name: Charles Michel
bio: President of the European Council from 2019 to 2024. Former Belgian PM.

name: António Costa
bio: President of the European Council since December 2024. Former PM of Portugal.

name: Roberta Metsola
bio: President of the European Parliament since 2022. Strong advocate for rule of law and anti-corruption.

name: Jens Stoltenberg
bio: NATO Secretary General from 2014 to October 2024. Led NATO through the Ukraine crisis and oversaw Finland and Sweden's accession.

name: Rafael Grossi
bio: Director General of the IAEA since 2019. Led nuclear inspections amid Iran tensions and monitored the Zaporizhzhia plant.

name: Moussa Faki Mahamat
bio: Chairperson of the AU Commission since 2017. Former PM and foreign minister of Chad.

name: Haitham al-Ghais
bio: Secretary General of OPEC since August 2022. Managed OPEC+ production cuts amid volatile energy markets.

name: Fatih Birol
bio: Executive Director of the IEA since 2015. One of the most influential voices on global energy transitions.

name: Dilma Rousseff
bio: President of the BRICS New Development Bank since 2023. Former President of Brazil (2011–2016).

name: Jerome Powell
bio: Chair of the Federal Reserve since 2018. Navigated the most aggressive rate-hiking cycle in four decades.

name: Christine Lagarde
bio: President of the ECB since 2019. Former IMF Managing Director. Steered eurozone policy through inflation and energy crises.

name: Andrew Bailey
bio: Governor of the Bank of England since 2020. Navigated British monetary policy through the pandemic and the 2022 gilt crisis.

name: Kazuo Ueda
bio: Governor of the Bank of Japan since April 2023. Ended the BOJ's negative interest rate policy and yield curve control.

name: Kristalina Georgieva
bio: IMF Managing Director since 2019. First person from an emerging market to lead the institution.

name: Ajay Banga
bio: World Bank President since June 2023. Former Mastercard CEO focused on climate and poverty.

name: Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala
bio: WTO Director-General since 2021. First woman and first African to lead the organization.

name: Janet Yellen
bio: U.S. Treasury Secretary from 2021 to 2025. Previously the first woman to chair the Federal Reserve (2014–2018).

name: Scott Bessent
bio: U.S. Treasury Secretary since January 2025. Former hedge fund manager and architect of Trump's economic policy.

name: Nirmala Sitharaman
bio: Finance Minister of India since 2019, the first full-time female finance minister.

name: Yi Gang
bio: Governor of the PBOC from 2018 to 2023. Managed monetary policy during economic transition.

name: Lloyd Austin
bio: U.S. Secretary of Defense from 2021 to January 2025. First African American to hold the position. Retired four-star Army general.

name: Pete Hegseth
bio: U.S. Secretary of Defense since January 2025. Former Fox News host and Army National Guard officer.

name: Sergei Shoigu
bio: Secretary of Russia's Security Council since May 2024. Served as Defence Minister from 2012 to 2024.

name: Andrei Belousov
bio: Defence Minister of Russia since May 2024. Economist whose appointment signaled focus on defense industry efficiency.

name: Valerii Zaluzhnyi
bio: Commander-in-Chief of Ukraine's Armed Forces from 2021 to February 2024. Led defense during the first two years of the full-scale invasion.

name: Oleksandr Syrskyi
bio: Commander-in-Chief of Ukraine's Armed Forces since February 2024. Led the defense of Kyiv and the Kharkiv counteroffensive.

name: Yoav Gallant
bio: Defense Minister of Israel from 2022 to November 2024. Oversaw military operations in Gaza following October 7.

name: Sébastien Lecornu
bio: French Minister of the Armed Forces since 2022. Overseeing France's military support to Ukraine.

name: Rajnath Singh
bio: Defence Minister of India since 2019. Overseeing defense modernization and the China border standoff.

name: Boris Pistorius
bio: German Defence Minister since January 2023. Pushing for Germany's 'Zeitenwende' in defense readiness.

name: Mahmoud Abbas
bio: President of the Palestinian Authority since 2005. Has pursued diplomatic recognition through international institutions.

name: Ismail Haniyeh
bio: Head of Hamas's political bureau from 2017 until his assassination in Tehran in July 2024.

name: Hassan Nasrallah
bio: Secretary-General of Hezbollah from 1992 until his killing in an Israeli airstrike in September 2024.

name: Abdul-Malik al-Houthi
bio: Leader of the Houthi movement since 2004. Launched Red Sea shipping attacks in late 2023 in solidarity with Gaza.

name: Lai Ching-te
bio: President of Taiwan since May 2024. DPP leader whose election China characterized as a 'dangerous choice.'

name: Tsai Ing-wen
bio: President of Taiwan from 2016 to May 2024. First female president. Deepened ties with democratic allies.

name: Pope Francis
bio: Head of the Catholic Church since 2013. First pope from the Americas. Focused on social justice, climate change, and interfaith dialogue.
